mnemonics: AWT = Abstract, Weighty, Troublesome. SWING = Sun's Wonderful Improvement for New GUIs.
keyPoints: Swing was created to overcome the limitations of AWT (Abstract Window Toolkit), which had platform-dependent and heavyweight components.
Swing introduced lightweight, pluggable look-and-feel components that rendered consistently across different operating systems.
It was released as part of the Java Foundation Classes (JFC) in 1997 and became a core part of Java SE.
commonMistakes: Confusing Swing with AWT and using them interchangeably, when they are distinct GUI toolkits.
Assuming Swing components are heavyweight like AWT, when they are actually lightweight and painted directly onto blank windows.
